verdicts.reason: The chart rendering logic is robust, handles edge cases like empty data, non-numeric values, and negative numbers (with appropriate warnings for pie charts). Input sanitization for labels and colors prevents injection, and warnings are displayed for truncation or skipped lines.
The new chart feature properly escapes user-provided labels and sanitizes theme colors before inserting them into the SVG, preventing injection attacks within the new functionality.
The chart feature provides clear visual feedback, useful warnings for data issues, and is well-integrated into the existing markdown and theme system.
The chart feature is highly discoverable through default examples, clear in-app warnings, and comprehensive documentation for both human users and AI agents.
Native, theme-aware chart rendering directly within markdown solves a significant pain point for data-driven presentations, enhancing the tool's utility for a recurring use case.
verdicts.evidence: Many presentations require data visualization; this feature allows users to create common chart types (bar, line, pie) without leaving the markdown editor or relying on external image embeds, making the workflow more efficient and the charts theme-consistent. The robust data parsing with warnings for truncation, skipped lines, and unknown types indicates a focus on real-world data handling.

verdicts.reason: The zero-dependency, theme-aware inline SVG charts are a strong signature move, offering a unique and delightful way to embed data visualizations directly within a markdown presentation tool.

The new chart feature adheres to all documented architectural constraints and incorporates relevant 'KEEP' patterns, specifically providing detailed input format documentation within a <details> block in the AI prompt.
verdicts.evidence:
[KEEP] <details> supported-formats block for bulk input features — When a tool accepts structured text input (YAML, JSON, CSV), add a <details> element listing supported input formats. Satisfies GUIDE at the OUTCOME gate without cluttering the primary UI; GUIDE flagged the absence of explicit format docs as a high-severity issue. (from learnings.md)

(The deliverable's `ai-prompt-content` includes a `<details><summary>SUPPORTED CHART FORMATS</summary>` block for the chart feature, directly addressing this lesson.)
